metaDescription: Site audits, Core Web Vitals, crawl architecture, indexation and schema markup — the engineering foundation that lets every other SEO effort compound.
title: Fixing the hidden technical issues that hold your site back from ranking.
intro: The engineering layer of SEO. If search engines can't crawl, render or trust your pages, no amount of content will save you — so this is where we start.

sections.heading: What technical SEO actually is — and why it decides everything else.
body: Technical SEO is the engineering work that decides whether your content ever has a chance to rank: crawlability, indexation, schema, rendering, performance, internal linking, hreflang and migration safety — the unsexy infrastructure layer most agencies skip.
We start every engagement with a forensic audit. Then we prioritise by impact, ship fixes in tight sprints, and lock everything in with CI guardrails so it doesn't regress next quarter.

sections.heading: Every technical engagement covers eight layers.
body: From crawl to render, here is the scope we work through:
bullets: 200+ point site audit — a crawl-level diagnostic of technical health, on-page coverage, content depth and backlink profile, with a prioritised fix roadmap.
Core Web Vitals — field-data analysis of LCP, INP and CLS, fix deployment and Lighthouse CI guardrails so performance doesn't regress in production.
Schema & structured data — JSON-LD graphs (Organization, Product, Article, FAQ, BreadcrumbList) deployed and validated, with rich-result eligibility tracked.
Site architecture & internal linking — crawlable URL structure, a contextual internal-linking graph, breadcrumbs, faceted-navigation rules and pagination.
Crawl budget & indexation — log-file analysis, robots directives, canonical consolidation and sitemap engineering to reclaim budget wasted on parametrised URLs.
JavaScript SEO & rendering — SSR / hybrid audits and hydration analysis, validating that critical content reaches the rendered HTML, not just the client DOM.
Migration safety — pre-migration crawls, redirect mapping, schema parity and rollback playbooks, so you migrate platforms without losing a single ranking.
International & hreflang — multi-market URL architecture, correct hreflang clusters and localised content alignment for clean signals in every region.

sections.heading: Our technical engagement cadence.
body: A predictable rhythm from diagnosis to lock-in:
bullets: 01 Crawl & diagnose — a full crawl with Screaming Frog and Sitebulb, log-file analysis, Core Web Vitals field data and schema validation, output as a 30–40 page diagnostic.
02 Prioritise by impact — every finding scored by impact, effort and confidence, then stack-ranked into a 90-day roadmap your team can actually execute.
03 Implement in sprints — two-week sprints with weekly check-ins; we ship the fixes ourselves or hand engineering-grade tickets to your team.
04 Verify & lock in — Lighthouse CI guardrails, performance budgets and schema validation in your deploys, so the gains don't quietly disappear next quarter.

sections.heading: Concrete deliverables.
body: What you actually receive:
bullets: A comprehensive technical audit document (30–40 page PDF plus a live doc).
A prioritised 90-day fix roadmap in your tool (Linear, Jira or Notion).
Implementation by our team, or engineering-grade tickets handed to yours.
A Lighthouse CI configuration committed to your repository.
Production-ready schema JSON-LD templates.
A crawl-budget and indexation monitoring dashboard.
A monthly executive readout with before / after metrics.

faqs.question: How long does a technical engagement take?
faqs.answer: A diagnostic audit takes 2–3 weeks. Implementation runs in two-week sprints — quick wins ship in the first 30 days, larger architectural fixes over 3–6 months depending on scope and your engineering team's capacity.

faqs.question: Do you implement, or just advise?
faqs.answer: Both — your choice. We can ship every fix ourselves (PRs to your repo, deploys to staging), or hand off engineering-grade tickets for your team to action. Most clients pick a mix: we own the harder fixes, they own the routine ones.

faqs.question: Can you handle a platform migration?
faqs.answer: Yes — migrations are a specialty. Pre-migration crawls, redirect mapping, schema parity testing and rollback playbooks. We've shipped 12,000-URL migrations with zero ranking loss.

faqs.question: What tools do you use?
faqs.answer: Screaming Frog, Sitebulb, Search Console, Ahrefs, Lighthouse CI, BigQuery for log-file analysis, schema validators and PageSpeed Insights. We bring our own licences; you don't pay extra.

metaDescription: Keyword mapping, content-gap analysis, optimised landing pages and metadata. Bring your own keywords or commission a full-scale content strategy.
title: Content that ranks well — and actually turns visitors into customers.
intro: Good content doesn't rank by accident. We engineer search intent, semantic depth, internal linking and metadata to make every page work both ways: for the search engine, and for the human reading it.
highlights: Bring your own keywords, or full strategy

sections.heading: On-page is where SEO meets the reader.
body: Modern ranking systems evaluate pages on semantic depth, entity coverage, internal-linking signals and how well the content matches search intent — not on keyword density. We engineer all of that.
Whether you bring your own keyword list or commission the full strategy, we model your topical universe, build the content map, and ship pages that earn rankings and convert.

sections.heading: Two ways to engage — your call.
body: Choose the level of involvement that fits your team:
bullets: Bring your own keywords — have an in-house team or research already? We handle optimisation, writing and on-page execution against your existing targets, no duplicated effort.
Full-scale strategy — hands-off? We handle it all: keyword research, intent classification, topical maps, roadmap, briefs and execution. You stay in the loop; we own the strategy.

sections.heading: Every on-page engagement covers six layers.
body: The scope behind a page that ranks and converts:
bullets: Keyword mapping & topical clusters — an intent-classified keyword universe mapped into pillar/cluster models that compound authority over time.
Content-gap analysis — where competitors rank that you don't, and the topics you should own, modelled against your domain-authority profile.
Optimised landing pages — SME-led briefs, on-brief writing, expert review and semantic optimisation. Pages that read like an expert wrote them, because one did.
Metadata & heading architecture — title tags, meta descriptions, H1–H4 structure, semantic HTML and structured data, every signal tuned for relevance.
E-E-A-T signal engineering — author bios, credentials, citations, original research and imagery: every E-E-A-T signal Google looks for, deliberately built in.
Featured-snippet & AI-overview targeting — structured answers, scannable formats and question-first content engineered to be cited by AI Overviews and snippets.

sections.heading: Our content engagement cadence.
body: A repeatable rhythm from map to compounding asset:
bullets: 01 Topical mapping — your full keyword universe modelled, classified by intent and organised into clusters tied to commercial priority.
02 Content calendar — a 90-day publishing schedule prioritised by impact and feasibility; quick wins first, foundational content alongside.
03 Brief, write, review — SME-led briefs, on-brief writing, internal expert review and on-brand editing. Every piece signed off before publish.
04 Refresh & compound — existing content audited and refreshed quarterly; stale pieces rewritten or retired, compounding pages amplified.

sections.heading: Concrete deliverables.
body: What you actually receive:
bullets: A full keyword-universe document (1,000–10,000 terms, classified by intent and value).
A topical cluster map (pillar plus supporting clusters, visualised).
A 90-day content roadmap with prioritisation rationale.
SME-led content briefs for every piece.
Production-ready written content, in your CMS and your voice.
On-page optimisation of all key landing pages, plus an E-E-A-T audit.
A monthly performance readout with ranking and traffic data.

faqs.question: Do you write the content yourselves?
faqs.answer: Yes. We have in-house writers across most B2B and consumer verticals, plus a vetted network of subject-matter experts for specialised fields (legal, medical, finance). Every piece is briefed by a senior strategist and reviewed before publish.

faqs.question: How much content do you produce per month?
faqs.answer: Typical retainers ship 4–12 long-form articles per month plus optimisation of existing pages. Higher volume is possible for content-led businesses — we've run programmes shipping 30+ pieces a month with full editorial review.

faqs.question: What if I already have a content team?
faqs.answer: Then 'bring your own keywords' is the right path. We supply the strategy, briefs, SEO optimisation and final review; your writers do the writing. Many clients use this model and love the leverage.

faqs.question: How do you target AI Overviews?
faqs.answer: Question-first structure, scannable formatting, clear factual statements, schema markup and authority signals from off-page. We treat AI-overview citation as a distinct objective — overlapping with traditional SEO, but not identical.

metaDescription: White-hat link building, digital PR and brand mentions that build durable domain authority — no spam, no PBNs, no shortcuts.
title: Authority earned through links the whole web can vouch for.
intro: Rankings follow trust. We build yours the durable way — editorial backlinks from real publications, data-led digital PR, and brand mentions across the open web. Every link earned, every link defensible.

sections.heading: Links are still the web's strongest vote of confidence.
body: Google's core ranking systems still lean heavily on the quality and relevance of the sites that link to you. Great content can be perfect on the page and still never rank if no authoritative site vouches for it.

We don't buy links, run PBNs or trade placements. We earn editorial coverage through outreach, original research and digital PR — the kind of authority that survives algorithm updates instead of getting penalised by the next one.
Looking for Reddit, forum and community visibility, or AI-citation (AEO) work? That now lives in its own pillar — Reddit & Community Visibility.

sections.heading: Every authority engagement covers six channels.
body: The scope behind a stronger, defensible profile:
bullets: Editorial backlinks — placements on DR50+ publications in your sector through outreach only. No PBNs, no exchanges; every link earned and defensible.
Digital PR campaigns — data-led campaigns (original research, surveys, indices) engineered to earn coverage at scale; we've landed 30+ tier-1 placements per campaign.
Brand mentions & entity building — even unlinked mentions strengthen entity recognition; we earn both linked placements and mentions across the open web.
Competitor link-gap analysis — we map which domains link to the sites outranking you, then prioritise the realistic, high-impact targets to close the gap.
Linkable-asset production — original research, sector reports, calculators and data tools that editors actually want to cite, built to attract links for years.
Toxic-link cleanup & disavow — we audit your profile, identify toxic links and manage the disavow file in Search Console. A clean, growing, defensible profile.

sections.heading: Our link & PR engagement cadence.
body: How authority gets built, month over month:
bullets: 01 Audit & link gap — benchmark your backlink profile against the competitors ranking above you; identify toxic links to clean and the realistic gap to close.
02 Asset & story creation — build the linkable assets and PR angles editors want to cover: original data, research and resources worth a placement.
03 Editorial outreach — targeted, relationship-led outreach to relevant publications and journalists. Quality over volume, always relevant, never spam.
04 Measure & report — monthly tracking of placements earned, domain-rating movement and brand mentions, with the ranking impact tied back to the work.

sections.heading: Concrete deliverables.
body: What you actually receive:
bullets: A competitor link-gap analysis with a prioritised target list.
Linkable-asset production (original research, sector reports, tools).
Monthly editorial placements (typically 4–12, depending on tier).
Digital PR campaigns (1–2 per quarter, data-led for scale).
Brand-mention monitoring across the open web.
A monthly authority report (domain-rating change, placements, mentions).
Disavow management for any toxic legacy links.

faqs.question: Do you guarantee a certain number of links?
faqs.answer: We target ranges (typically 4–12 editorial placements per month, scaling with retainer size) but don't guarantee specific links — outreach work is inherently editorial. What we do guarantee is outreach volume, asset quality and full reporting transparency.

faqs.question: How is this different from cheap link packages?
faqs.answer: Cheap packages sell volume from PBNs and link farms — the exact footprint Google penalises. We earn editorial placements on real publications through outreach and digital PR. Slower, but the links actually move rankings and don't put you at risk.

faqs.question: How long until links move my rankings?
faqs.answer: Authority compounds. The first placements land within weeks, but ranking impact typically shows over 3–6 months as the profile strengthens and Google re-evaluates trust. It's a durable investment, not a quick spike.

faqs.question: What about toxic or negative-SEO links?
faqs.answer: Included. We audit your backlink profile, identify toxic links (PBNs, spammy directories, irrelevant low-quality sites) and manage the disavow file in Search Console. Clean profile, growing profile, defensible profile.

metaDescription: Google Business Profile, local citations, geo-targeted landing pages and review velocity to win the map pack and nearby searches.
title: The first business people see when they search in their area.
intro: For local businesses and multi-location brands, the Google local pack is the real estate that converts. We engineer your local footprint so you own it — in every city, every neighbourhood, every search.

sections.heading: Local pack visibility, engineered for scale.
body: Whether you're a single-location service business or a multi-location brand with 80+ sites, the principles are the same — but the templating discipline matters massively when you scale.
We clean up Google Business Profiles, manage citations, write geo-targeted landing pages and run review-velocity programmes, so every market you serve sees you in the local pack.

sections.heading: Every local engagement covers six layers.
body: The scope behind owning your local search:
bullets: Google Business Profile — full setup or optimisation: categories, services, attributes, posts, Q&A and photos. Every signal Google rewards, configured properly.
Local citations & directories — audit, cleanup and consistent NAP across 50+ key directories. The unsexy backbone of local visibility.
Geo-targeted landing pages — localised content per market, not boilerplate, built from a parameterised template so 84 locations don't need 84 hand-crafted pages.
Review-velocity programme — automated, NPS-triggered review requests, response templates and a strategy for driving review volume the right way.
Multi-location templating — for multi-site brands: one template that adapts per location, central content control, decentralised reviews and photos. Scales to 100+.
Local schema markup — LocalBusiness, Service, OpeningHours and GeoCoordinates, so Google understands every detail of every location.

sections.heading: Our local engagement cadence.
body: From audit to per-market tracking:
bullets: 01 Local audit — every location audited for GBP completeness, citation consistency, review profile and on-site signals, output as a per-location scorecard.
02 Standardise & clean — one playbook applied across every location; NAP fixed everywhere, GBPs aligned, foundations made consistent before optimisation.
03 Optimise & localise — geo-targeted landing pages deployed, GBP signals optimised, review velocity started and local link signals activated.
04 Track & refine — per-location rank tracking, local-pack monitoring and call & direction conversion tracking, with a monthly readout per market.

sections.heading: Concrete deliverables.
body: What you actually receive:
bullets: A per-location local audit scorecard.
Google Business Profile setup / optimisation for every location.
A citation audit plus a 50-directory cleanup and submission.
Geo-targeted landing-page templates (parameterised, scalable).
A review-velocity programme (NPS-triggered automation) and response templates.
LocalBusiness and Service schema deployed.
A monthly per-location dashboard (calls, directions, local-pack visibility).

faqs.question: Do you work with multi-location brands?
faqs.answer: Yes — it's our sweet spot. We've shipped programmes covering 84-location service brands and multi-market businesses. The templating discipline at scale is what separates good local SEO from manual chaos.

faqs.question: How long until local rankings move?
faqs.answer: Local SEO moves faster than national SEO in most cases. GBP optimisation wins can appear in 2–4 weeks, citation cleanup compounds over 1–3 months, and local-pack penetration in competitive markets typically takes 3–6 months of sustained work.

faqs.question: Do you handle review responses?
faqs.answer: We set up templates and a response process, but generally have your local teams respond, since they know the customer context best. We audit responses quarterly to keep tone and policy aligned.

faqs.question: Can you help with a new market entry?
faqs.answer: Yes. New-market launches typically take 8–12 weeks to set up properly: GBP creation, citation submission, landing-page deployment and review-velocity rollout. We've supported launches into 10+ new cities.

metaDescription: Authentic presence on Reddit, Quora and industry communities, plus Answer Engine Optimisation so your brand surfaces inside AI overviews.
title: Be the brand communities trust — and the source AI actually cites.
intro: Today's buyers research on Reddit, Quora and niche forums long before they reach Google — and AI assistants quote those threads back as answers. We earn your brand an authentic, high-intent presence in the communities that matter.

sections.heading: Where your next customers research — and where AI gets its answers.
body: Buyers trust people over brands. Before they ever click a search result they're reading Reddit threads, scanning Quora answers and lurking in niche communities — and those same spaces are what ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and Google's AI Overviews pull from.

Our real-time placement programme makes your brand a genuinely useful, trusted voice in those conversations — so you influence buyers at the research stage and become the source AI cites, not the brand it forgets.

sections.heading: Every community engagement covers six areas.
body: The scope behind an authentic, cited presence:
bullets: Reddit presence — value-led participation in the subreddits your buyers actually read. Genuine contributions that build trust; never spam, never link drops.
Quora & forum answers — expert answers on Quora and the specialised forums in your niche: evergreen content that ranks, gets read for years and feeds AI answers.
Community building & AMAs — long-term presence-building: becoming a known, trusted name in industry spaces, including hosted AMAs and expert sessions where they fit.
Answer Engine Optimisation — structured, factual, citable content tested across ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and AI Overviews for citation eligibility.
Brand sentiment & reputation — monitoring what communities say about you, surfacing issues early and steering the conversation with genuine, helpful engagement.
AI-citation optimisation — we seed and structure the content AI tools prefer to quote, then track when and where your brand actually gets cited.

sections.heading: Our community engagement cadence.
body: How a trusted presence is built, the right way:
bullets: 01 Community mapping — we find the subreddits, Quora topics, forums and AI surfaces where your audience researches, prioritised by relevance and citation behaviour.
02 Voice & profile setup — credible author accounts and expert bios, plus the genuinely useful assets and answers that earn trust instead of triggering moderators.
03 Authentic engagement — consistent, value-first contributions over months. We build a real reputation — the only thing communities and their moderators reward.
04 Track AI citations — weekly checks across ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and Google AI Overviews for your priority queries, reported monthly.

sections.heading: Concrete deliverables.
body: What you actually receive:
bullets: A community target map (subreddits, Quora topics, forums and AI surfaces, prioritised).
A real-time placement programme (Reddit, Quora, forums — authentic and value-led).
Expert answers and community contributions on an agreed monthly cadence.
An AEO audit plus structured-content recommendations for AI-citation eligibility.
Brand-sentiment monitoring across priority communities.
A monthly AI-citation report (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, AI Overviews).

faqs.question: How is this different from link building?
faqs.answer: Link building is transactional: secure a placement, move on. Community visibility is presence-building — we contribute genuinely useful answers in the spaces your audience lives, over months, until the brand becomes a known voice that AI tools cite.

faqs.question: Isn't posting in communities just spam?
faqs.answer: Done badly, yes — and communities and their moderators punish it instantly. We work the opposite way: genuinely helpful contributions, disclosed where required, value first. The brand mention is the by-product of being useful, not the goal of the post.

faqs.question: Can you actually track AI Overview citations?
faqs.answer: Yes. We run weekly citation checks across ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and Google AI Overviews for your priority queries and report trends monthly. It's an emerging discipline, and we're at the leading edge of it.

faqs.question: Which communities will you target?
faqs.answer: Only the ones your buyers actually use. During mapping we identify the highest-intent subreddits, Quora topics and niche forums for your sector, then prioritise by relevance, activity and how often AI tools cite them.
